name: Esteval dos Mouros Trail
name_original: Esteval dos Mouros — percurso circular (LLE PR)
description: Hike the moderate Esteval dos Mouros Trail, a 5.4-mile route featuring the Barrocal Red Dirt Road and the Abandoned Village of Rocha Amarela
region_intro: Hiking around Alte, a village in the Algarve region, offers diverse terrain ranging from fertile Barrocal to the mountainous Serra do Caldeirão. The landscape is characterized by its hilly countryside, natural springs, and varied vegetation including forests and grasslands. Notable natural features include the Fonte Grande de Alte and the Vigário Waterfall, alongside traditional villages and agricultural areas. The region's elevation provides scenic views, with the Rocha da Pena Local Protected Landscape reaching 479 meters. Best hiking trails around Alte The most popular hiking route is Esteval dos Mouros Trail, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 25 minutes to complete, offering views of the surrounding Algarve hills. Another top favourite among local hikers is Bike Café - Germano – Fonte Grande de Alte loop from Alte, an easy 4.2 miles (6.8 km) path. This route passes by the natural springs and traditional village architecture. Local hikers also love the Fontes Village – Fonte Grande de Alte loop from Alte, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail leading through the countryside and past natural spring features, often completed in about 2 hours 21 minutes. Hiking around Alte is defined by hilly landscapes, natural springs, and traditional villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Alte are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 2,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Alte's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Alte?
A: Alte offers a diverse network of over 30 hiking trails. These routes range from easy walks suitable for families to more challenging paths, allowing you to explore the region's hilly landscapes, natural springs, and traditional villages.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Alte?
A: Yes, Alte has several easy trails perfect for families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10291094" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike Café - Germano – Fonte Grande de Alte loop from Alte&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.2-mile (6.8 km) path that passes by natural springs and traditional architecture, offering a pleasant experience for all ages.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls near Alte?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its natural water features. The Vigário Waterfall (Cascata Queda do Vigário) is a prominent natural highlight located about 1.2 km from Alte. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/460116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrocal Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4761400" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algribre Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; along some routes, especially after rainfall.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Alte?
A: Many of the trails around Alte are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10291094" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike Café - Germano – Fonte Grande de Alte loop from Alte&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/702951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontes Village – Fonte Grande de Alte loop from Alte&lt;/a&gt;, which both offer scenic loops through the countryside.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see while hiking in Alte?
A: Alte's trails lead to various natural attractions. You can visit the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/568312" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonte Grande de Alte&lt;/a&gt;, a natural spring with a developed bathing area. Other highlights include the Vigário Waterfall, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/688521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Crest Trail of Rocha da Pena&lt;/a&gt;, and several viewpoints offering panoramic vistas of the Algarve hills.

Q: Is Alte a good destination for challenging hikes?
A: While many routes are moderate or easy, Alte does offer some more challenging options. The region's hilly landscape, situated between the Barrocal and the mountainous Serra do Caldeirão, provides trails with significant elevation changes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32177382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontes Village – Rocha dos Soidos Summit loop from Alte&lt;/a&gt;, which features notable ascents.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Alte?
A: The hiking trails in Alte are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse scenery, the tranquility of the countryside, and the charm of passing through traditional villages and by natural springs.

Q: Are there any good spots for wild swimming near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/568312" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonte Grande de Alte&lt;/a&gt; is a popular spot for wild swimming. It features a beautiful natural pool fed by crystal-clear spring water, making it an ideal place to cool off during or after a hike, especially on warm days.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alte?
A: The best time to hike in Alte is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder, the landscape is lush, and the natural springs and waterfalls are more likely to be flowing. Summers can be very hot, and some waterfalls may dry up.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or panoramic spots along the trails?
A: Yes, the hilly terrain around Alte offers numerous viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/219453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trig Point and Panoramic View at Rocha da Pena&lt;/a&gt; is a significant landmark, providing expansive views. Other spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6378366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Grande Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; also offer stunning vistas of the surrounding Algarve hills and countryside.

Q: Is it possible to reach the hiking trails in Alte using public transport?
A: Alte is a village in the Algarve, and while public transport options exist, they might be limited compared to larger towns. It's advisable to check local bus schedules in advance if you plan to rely on public transport to reach the trailheads. Many visitors opt for personal vehicles for greater flexibility.

Q: Where can I park my car when hiking in Alte?
A: Parking is generally available within or near the village of Alte. Specific parking information for individual trailheads might vary, but you can usually find designated parking areas in the village center or at popular starting points for routes. It's recommended to arrive early during peak seasons.
tips: Along the route there are reddish dirt roads. The composition of the soil is responsible for the hue, which is rarely observed on the European continent.

The village of Rocha Amarela is located along the Esteval dos Mouros route. It is a village that has been completely abandoned for over 50 years. Despite its advanced state of dilapidation, it is an interesting place to explore.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Esteval dos Mouros Trail?
faqs.answer: The Esteval dos Mouros Trail is a moderate hike that generally takes around 2 hours and 25 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. It involves some elevation gain and varied terrain, making it suitable for hikers with a reasonable level of fitness. While not overly strenuous, it's more challenging than a simple stroll.

faqs.question: Is the Esteval dos Mouros Trail a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Esteval dos Mouros Trail is a circular route, meaning you will end up back at your starting point without needing to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for the Esteval dos Mouros Trail are not provided in the available data. It is advisable to check local maps or community forums for designated parking areas near the start of the route before your visit.

faqs.question: What can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the Esteval dos Mouros Trail, you'll encounter interesting features such as the Barrocal Red Dirt Road, which offers a distinct landscape. You can also explore the historic Abandoned Village of Rocha Amarela, providing a glimpse into the region's past.

faqs.question: Is the Esteval dos Mouros Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this trail are not detailed, generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in Portugal, especially in rural areas. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash for their safety and to respect wildlife and other hikers. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Esteval dos Mouros Trail. It appears to be freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike in the Algarve region, where this trail is located, is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the natural scenery is often at its most vibrant, avoiding the intense heat of summer.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with any other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Esteval dos Mouros Trail intersects with other routes. Notably, approximately 49% of this route also follows sections of the official Via Algarviana - Ligação 7: Albufeira-Alte trail.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: As a moderate hike, you can expect varied terrain. While specific details are limited, the presence of a "Barrocal Red Dirt Road" highlight suggests unpaved, possibly uneven paths. Be prepared for natural surfaces, which may include dirt, gravel, and some rocky sections, typical of rural Portuguese trails.

faqs.question: Is the Esteval dos Mouros Trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating and a duration of about 2 hours and 25 minutes, the trail might be suitable for active families with older children who are accustomed to hiking. It may be too challenging for very young children or those not used to longer walks with elevation changes.
